frameworks.name: proactive adaptation / predictive coding allocation
frameworks.one_line: Adaptation reallocates coding resources to optimize encoding of the next expected input

frameworks.name: information-theoretic encoding analysis
frameworks.one_line: Discrimination thresholds are converted into encoding accuracy via Fisher information

frameworks.name: natural-scene statistics of retinal input
frameworks.one_line: Temporal statistics of orientations in free-viewing retinal streams predict adapted encoding

frameworks.name: PredNet recurrent predictive network
frameworks.one_line: RNN trained to predict next video frame is used as a model organism for adaptation

core_question: Does sensory adaptation proactively optimize encoding for the next expected stimulus rather than merely reacting to recent history?
key_assumptions: Total coding capacity is conserved across adaptation states
Discrimination thresholds index local encoding accuracy
Short-window orientation history predicts the next retinal input distribution
manipulations: Natural retinal input from freely behaving subjects vs lab stimuli
Training PredNet on natural-scene video to predict next frame
key_findings: Adaptation reallocates coding resources so accuracy peaks at the adaptor orientation
Total coding capacity remains approximately constant across adaptation states
Local retinal orientation distribution peaks at the mean orientation over recent history
PredNet trained on next-frame prediction reproduces the adaptation signature
interpretations: Adaptation acts proactively, tuning encoding toward the next expected sensory input
Reactive adaptation history can be reinterpreted as a prediction about upcoming input
